response_body_utf8: {"status":"ok","message-type":"work","message-version":"1.0.0","message":{"indexed":{"date-parts":[[2026,6,23]],"date-time":"2026-06-23T16:49:21Z","timestamp":1782233361408,"version":"3.54.5"},"reference-count":16,"publisher":"Sociedade Brasileira de Computa\u00e7\u00e3o - SBC","content-domain":{"domain":[],"crossmark-restriction":false},"short-container-title":[],"abstract":"<jats:p>Dermatological AI models frequently fail on darker skin tones, posing significant safety and regulatory risks. To prevent unsafe out-of-distribution inference, we propose an interpretable Fitzpatrick Skin Type (FST) classifier that acts as an active algorithmic guardrail. Our Prototype Matching framework classifies clinical images into FST I-IV or V-VI. To ensure robustness, we employ SAM3 pre-segmentation to isolate healthy skin prior to feature extraction. This strictly prevents shortcut learning caused by clinical artifacts and lesion morphology. Evaluated on the Diverse Dermatology Images dataset, our method achieved a Cohen\u2019s Kappa of 0.78, Balanced Accuracy of 0.88, and Macro F1-Score of 0.89, substantially outperforming established baselines. By retrieving the nearest prototypes for visual verification, our framework delivers the interpretability required for safe, compliant medical AI deployment.<\/jats:p>","DOI":"10.5753\/sbcas.2026.21435","type":"proceedings-article","created":{"date-parts":[[2026,6,23]],"date-time":"2026-06-23T15:18:18Z","timestamp":1782227898000},"page":"657-668","source":"Crossref","is-referenced-by-count":0,"title":["Enabling safe AI deployment: an automated Fitzpatrick skin type guardrail for out-of-distribution dermatology"],"prefix":"10.5753","author":[{"given":"Pedro H.
